title: stachys palustris var. aspera
intro: Stachys palustris var. aspera, also known as rough hedge nettle or rough marsh hedge nettle, is a plant species within the mint family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Stachys palustris var. aspera often include infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
